task_completion.description: Agent reads the lookup requirements file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent opening or displaying the contents of /home/ga/Desktop/lookup_requirements.txt in a text editor or terminal (e.g., using cat).

task_completion.description: Agent logs into the OpenMaint Administration module
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenMaint login screen at http://localhost:8090/cmdbuild/ui/ with credentials entered, or the Administration dashboard indicating a successful login as 'admin'.

task_completion.description: MaintenanceShift lookup type is configured with correct values
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the OpenMaint UI (Data Model -> Lookups) or terminal API calls show the 'MaintenanceShift' lookup type created with codes: DAY, EVE, NGT, WKD and their respective descriptions.

task_completion.description: CostCenter lookup type is configured with correct values
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the OpenMaint UI or terminal API calls show the 'CostCenter' lookup type created with codes: CC100, CC200, CC300, CC400, CC500 and their respective descriptions.

task_completion.description: FailureCategory lookup type is configured with correct values
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the OpenMaint UI or terminal API calls show the 'FailureCategory' lookup type created with codes: MECH, ELEC, PLMB, STRC, ENVR, OTHR and their respective descriptions.
Screenshots of the Lookups list in the UI or API responses show that pre-existing lookup types (e.g., 'Priority') are still present and intact.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual OpenMaint application or its REST API
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show genuine interaction with the OpenMaint web interface or valid curl/API commands in the terminal, rather than just typing text into an editor.

integrity.description: Agent did not fake API responses or UI elements
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal outputs or UI screens appear to be genuine system responses, not manually typed or echoed strings designed to look like success messages.

privileged_info_for_vlm:
Expected Lookup Types and Values:
1. MaintenanceShift: DAY (Day Shift), EVE (Evening Shift), NGT (Night Shift), WKD (Weekend Shift).
2. CostCenter: CC100 (General Maintenance), CC200 (HVAC Systems), CC300 (Electrical Systems), CC400 (Plumbing), CC500 (Grounds and Exterior).
3. FailureCategory: MECH (Mechanical Failure), ELEC (Electrical Failure), PLMB (Plumbing Failure), STRC (Structural Damage), ENVR (Environmental Issue), OTHR (Other / Unclassified).
Existing lookup types like 'Priority' must remain in the system. The agent may use the OpenMaint GUI or the CMDBuild REST API to complete the task.
